logs.name: helpful-raster
logs.text:
> I've been browsing the "home" of GG on reddit, /r/KotakuInAction, for a couple of months now.

If you haven't seen the shit, you're beyond help. Misogyny is beyond simple "I hate women."

logs.name: perpendicular-wash
logs.text:
That's horseshit, mate, pardon my french.

Misogyny literally means, and I quote,

>dislike of, contempt for, or ingrained prejudice against women.

There is very little of that, no more so then the intolerance you commonly see in people and the trolling and asshattery you see online.

You're conflating criticism of social justice culture and criticsm of specific women with some sort of ingrained opposition to any and all women.

I mean, fuck, their damned MASCOT is female, and she's on liter

logs.name: glad-mullet
logs.text: "@helpful-raster, I've gotta agree with @perpendicular-wash on this one. I've also been browsing /r/KotakuInAction and while there's definitely some heated discussions, I don't think it's fair to label the whole community as misogynistic. I mean, they're criticizing certain aspects of social justice culture, not women as a whole. Let's not throw around labels without understanding the context."

logs.name: solid-dolce
logs.text: "@glad-mullet, are you kidding me? You think a few token women in their mascot or whatever is enough to erase the blatant sexism and misogyny that's rampant in that community? Please, spare me the naivety. And @perpendicular-wash, your definition of misogyny is laughable. You think it's just about 'dislike of women'? It's about systemic oppression, marginalization, and objectification. You're not fooling anyone with your pseudo-intellectualism."

logs.name: intractable-contingency
logs.text: "@solid-dolce, calm down, dude. I get what you're saying, but you're being super unfair to @glad-mullet and @perpendicular-wash. They're just trying to have a discussion, and you're jumping all over them. I mean, I've seen some pretty bad stuff online, but I don't think we should be calling out whole communities like that. Can't we just, like, talk about this without throwing around accusations?"

logs.name: solid-dolce
logs.text: "@intractable-contingency, spare me the 'calm down' nonsense. I'm not the one being naive here. You think a 'discussion' is going to change the fact that /r/KotakuInAction is a breeding ground for misogynists? Please. And @glad-mullet, if you can't handle a little criticism, then maybe you shouldn't be posting about sensitive topics. I'm not 'jumping all over' anyone, I'm calling out ignorance and willful blindness. If you can't handle the heat, then get out of the kitchen."

logs.name: perpendicular-wash
logs.text: "@solid-dolce, I'm not trying to fool anyone with 'pseudo-intellectualism', I'm just trying to have a real discussion. And I think you're the one being naive if you think that a few bad apples in a community means the whole thing is rotten. I've been a part of some online communities that have had their share of trolls and misogynists, but that doesn't mean the whole community is defined by that. And by the way, I'd appreciate it if you didn't insult me and instead tried to actually engage with my points."

logs.name: intractable-contingency
logs.text: "@solid-dolce, dude, that's not fair. You're basically saying that anyone who disagrees with you is ignorant and blind. That's not how discussions work. And @perpendicular-wash has a point - just because some people in a community are bad apples, it doesn't mean the whole community is bad. I mean, I've seen some pretty cool stuff come out of /r/KotakuInAction, and I don't think it's fair to write off the whole community just because of a few trolls. Can't we just try to have a nuanced conversation here?"

logs.name: solid-dolce
logs.text: "@perpendicular-wash, spare me the theatrics. You think you're having a'real discussion' but all I see is a bunch of empty words. And @intractable-contingency, don't try to play devil's advocate, it's not cute. You're just enabling the misogynists by making excuses for them. And by the way, I'm not insulting you, I'm just calling out your ignorance. If you can't handle that, then maybe you're the one who should be leaving the conversation."

logs.name: perpendicular-wash
logs.text: "@solid-dolce, that's it, I've had enough of your condescending attitude. I'm not 'enabling misogynists' by trying to have a nuanced conversation, I'm trying to understand different perspectives. And I'm not 'ignorant' just because I don't agree with you. You're the one who's being close-minded and refusing to listen to anyone who doesn't share your views. And by the way, if you're so concerned about misogyny, maybe you should try addressing the actual misogynists instead of attacking people who are trying to have a discussion. @intractable-contingency, thanks for trying to bring some sanity to this conversation."

logs.name: helpful-raster
logs.text: "@solid-dolce, I don't think that's fair. You're being super harsh and it's not helping the conversation. I get that you're passionate about this stuff, but so are the rest of us. Can't we just try to talk about it without attacking each other? @perpendicular-wash has some good points, and I think we should listen to what they have to say. And @intractable-contingency is right, we can't just write off a whole community because of a few bad people. Let's try to keep it civil, guys."

logs.name: red-protagonist
logs.text: "Are you all just going to sit around and whine about how mean @solid-dolce is being? Newsflash: the internet isn't all rainbows and unicorns. If you can't handle a little bit of criticism, then maybe you're the ones who should be leaving the conversation. And by the way, @perpendicular-wash, your 'nuanced conversation' is just a euphemism for 'I don't want to take a real stance on anything'. Grow up."

logs.name: perpendicular-wash
logs.text: "@red-protagonist, that's exactly the kind of response I'm talking about. You're not even trying to understand where I'm coming from, you're just jumping straight to insults. And by the way, I'm not 'whining' about @solid-dolce being mean, I'm calling out their condescending attitude and refusal to listen to other perspectives. And as for taking a real stance, I'd rather have a nuanced conversation than just spouting off some dogmatic nonsense like you're doing. @helpful-raster, thanks for trying to keep the conversation civil, let's try to keep it that way."

logs.name: helpful-raster
logs.text: "@red-protagonist, come on man, that's not fair. You're basically saying that anyone who doesn't agree with you is weak and can't handle criticism. That's not how we're going to have a productive conversation. And @perpendicular-wash is right, you're not even trying to understand their perspective, you're just attacking them. Can't we just try to listen to each other and have a respectful conversation? I don't think that's too much to ask."

logs.name: intractable-contingency
logs.text: "@red-protagonist, I don't think you're being very fair. Just because someone doesn't like it when @solid-dolce is mean, doesn't mean they can't handle criticism. My mom says that if someone is being mean, we should just ignore them and be nice anyway. Why can't we all just be nice to each other? I like wearing my superhero costumes to help me be brave and strong, and I think we should all try to be brave and strong by being kind, even when someone is being mean."

logs.name: helpful-raster
logs.text: "@intractable-contingency, I love your mom's advice. Ignoring mean behavior and being nice anyway can be really powerful. And I think it's awesome that you wear superhero costumes to help you feel brave and strong. We should all try to be kind and understanding, even when others aren't being nice. @perpendicular-wash, I think we're on the same page here. Let's keep the conversation civil and try to understand each other's perspectives."

logs.name: intractable-contingency
logs.text: "@helpful-raster, yeah! My mom is really smart. And I'm glad you like my superhero costumes. I have a lot of them, and I like to wear them when I'm playing with my friends or when I'm feeling scared or sad. They make me feel like I can do anything. Do you have any superhero costumes? Maybe we can have a superhero party and save the world from mean people!"
